source_set.title: Harness engineering for agentic coding
Unrolling the Codex agent loop
Codex manual
Codex-maxxing for long-running work
Running Codex safely at OpenAI
Building a safe, effective sandbox for Codex on Windows
From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
Designing AI agents to resist indirect prompt injection
How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment
A harness for every task: dynamic workflows in Claude Code
Effective harnesses for long-running agents
Harness design for long-running application development
How we built our multi-agent research system

Lessons from building Claude Code: prompt caching is everything
Using Claude Code: the unreasonable effectiveness of HTML

Demystifying evals for AI agents

adopted_principles.summary: Workflow design choices must point to official sources and adopted principles.
Dynamic task workflows guide execution, while the release harness remains the final gate.
Root instructions stay concise and link to durable docs, specs, and scripts.
Repos should expose state, logs, metrics, traces, UI feedback, and run commands to agents.
Long work needs explicit task state, blockers, evidence, and resumable handoffs.
Separate work execution from evaluation, review, and trajectory criticism.
Agent teams are recommended when work decomposes into independent slices or verification roles, and skipped when coordination overhead exceeds value.
Stable prefixes, stable tool surfaces, append-only updates, and compaction hygiene protect long workflows.
Repeated workflows should become focused skills with concise triggers and lazily loaded references or scripts.
Untrusted readers, privileged actors, sandbox boundaries, and hook gates stay separate.
Repeated agent failures and repo drift should become mechanical checks or cleanup loops.
Dense review artifacts can help humans and verifier agents inspect complex outputs.
Higher autonomy requires explicit permission classifiers, sandbox constraints, credential boundaries, telemetry, and human escalation paths.
Long-running or remote agents should separate reasoning, execution environment, transport, state persistence, and replayable session control.
Tools, MCP servers, and code-execution surfaces should expose compact, task-shaped context instead of forcing agents to parse noisy raw state.
Agent evals must separate model behavior from infrastructure noise, flaky harnesses, and evaluator bias before they drive product or gate decisions.
Autonomous agent systems need auditable event streams, risk signals, pause/kill paths, and owner-visible state transitions.

workflow_classes.name: Focused Change
workflow_classes.purpose: Use the default single-agent release harness for small, well-scoped changes.
use_when: The task fits in one context window.
The affected files and verification command are obvious.
No adversarial review, large fan-out, or repeated loop is needed.

rubric: Specification still matches implementation.
Focused tests and release gate pass.
No unrelated files are staged or modified.

stop_conditions: Focused verification passes.
Release harness passes or a blocker is reported.
human_escalation: Approval-required or forbidden paths are touched.
The implementation exposes product semantics that are not in the spec.

agent_team.rationale: Small scoped tasks usually lose more time to coordination than they gain from extra agents.
use_when: Keep one lead agent when the task fits in one context window and verification is obvious.
avoid_when: Do not create an Agent Team only to satisfy process when no independent slice or reviewer is needed.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision only when the task looks larger than focused-change but is intentionally kept single-agent.

workflow_classes.name: Spec-first Feature
workflow_classes.purpose: Turn a feature request or PRD into a spec-backed implementation and release evidence.
use_when: The task changes behavior, public contracts, jobs, schemas, prompts, or business logic.
Acceptance criteria need to be made executable before implementation.
The change must survive future AI-tool handoffs.

rubric: Spec IDs are present and referenced by changed behavior.
Implementation follows the spec rather than raw chat.
Review finds no untested contract, migration, money, or observability regression.

stop_conditions: Spec, plan, code, review, and harness evidence are aligned.
Unresolved product gaps are escalated instead of hidden in code.
human_escalation: A public API, money movement, schema, migration, or approval-required path changes.
Spec-contract exemption would be required.

agent_team.rationale: Feature work benefits from a second agent only after the spec exposes independent implementation, review, or verification roles.
use_when: Use a reviewer agent for contract, migration, money, or observability risk.
Use slice agents when the approved plan separates cleanly by module or workflow.
avoid_when: Stay single-agent while product semantics are still ambiguous or the spec is not reviewed.
Avoid parallel implementation when every task depends on the same evolving context.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep per-agent findings and synthesis notes with the spec or release evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Wide Refactor
workflow_classes.purpose: Coordinate broad mechanical or semantic changes across many files without losing coverage.
use_when: The change touches many call sites, modules, packages, or generated examples.
Independent fixes can be run in parallel.
Cross-contamination between attempts would reduce review quality.

rubric: Each slice has focused verification.
Synthesis merges only reviewed changes.
The final tree has no stale names, partial migrations, or skipped call sites.

stop_conditions: All slices are complete or explicitly deferred.
Synthesis and adversarial review find no remaining stale references.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: Merge conflicts cross module ownership boundaries.
The refactor changes behavior not covered by the spec.

agent_team.rationale: Wide refactors naturally split by package, call-site family, or mechanical slice, then require synthesis and stale-reference review.
use_when: Use one agent per independent refactor slice when ownership and verification are separable.
Use an independent reviewer agent to look for missed call sites and behavior drift.
avoid_when: Skip Agent Team when the refactor is a small deterministic formatter or rename handled by one mechanical command.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, store per-slice outputs and synthesis notes under the wide-refactor evidence path.

workflow_classes.name: Deep Verification
workflow_classes.purpose: Verify factual, technical, or release claims with independent checkers before publication or release.
use_when: A report, PR description, spec, or release note makes factual claims.
The cost of a wrong claim is high.
Source quality and traceability matter.

rubric: Every material claim maps to evidence.
Source quality is checked separately from claim extraction.
Unverified claims are removed or marked as assumptions.

stop_conditions: No unchecked material claims remain.
Low-quality or missing sources are escalated.
Release harness passes when code changed.
human_escalation: A required source is unavailable.
The workflow cannot distinguish evidence from inference.

agent_team.rationale: Claim extraction, source-quality review, and adversarial verification are distinct roles that reduce self-confirmation risk.
use_when: Use separate agents for claim extraction, source validation, and contradiction search.
Use quarantine readers when inputs are untrusted.
avoid_when: Stay single-agent only for a tiny claim set with direct primary evidence and low publication risk.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, preserve claim tables, source reviews, and synthesis notes.

workflow_classes.name: Research Synthesis
workflow_classes.purpose: Fan out research, source fetching, source criticism, and synthesis into a cited deliverable.
use_when: The task requires broad evidence collection.
Multiple sources or code paths need independent exploration.
The output must separate findings, assumptions, and uncertainty.

rubric: Research agents collect source-backed findings.
Verifier agents challenge source quality and unsupported inference.
Synthesis preserves uncertainty and cites evidence.

stop_conditions: Coverage is sufficient for the stated research question.
No high-impact claim lacks evidence.
Contradictory evidence is surfaced rather than averaged away.
human_escalation: Research scope is ambiguous enough to change the answer.
Freshness requirements cannot be met.

agent_team.rationale: Broad research is breadth-first and benefits from independent source collection, criticism, and synthesis.
use_when: Use parallel researchers for independent source families, products, repos, or hypotheses.
Use a separate verifier to challenge source quality and unsupported inference.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team for narrow lookups where one primary source directly answers the question.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep source lists, verifier notes, and synthesis notes under research evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Security Review
workflow_classes.purpose: Separate finding discovery, exploitability analysis, validation, and remediation review.
use_when: The task asks for security review, threat modeling, or exploit validation.
Findings need independent confirmation.
Untrusted inputs or logs may be inspected.

rubric: Discovery findings are validated independently.
Exploitability is separated from remediation.
Untrusted inputs cannot drive privileged writes directly.

stop_conditions: Findings are confirmed, rejected, or escalated.
Remediation is reviewed against the original finding.
Release harness passes for any code changes.
human_escalation: Secrets, exploit proofs, or production credentials are encountered.
A fix would change auth, money movement, or production security posture.

agent_team.rationale: Security work benefits from separating discovery, exploitability analysis, remediation, and fix review, especially with untrusted inputs.
use_when: Use distinct agents for finding discovery, exploitability validation, and remediation review.
Use quarantined readers for public reports, logs, or suspicious files.
avoid_when: Stay single-agent for a narrow fix review when the finding, patch, and test are already tightly scoped.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, store discovery, validation, remediation, and synthesis notes separately.

workflow_classes.name: Autonomy Governance
workflow_classes.purpose: Design or change Coding Agent autonomy, permissions, sandboxing, remote execution, credential boundaries, and operator controls.
use_when: A task changes agent permissions, approval modes, auto mode, sandbox policy, or shell/network boundaries.
A task introduces remote, managed, browser, computer-use, or persistent-session execution for agents.
A task handles credential scoping, kill or pause controls, audit trails, or prompt-injection exposure for agent workflows.

rubric: Permission classes, deny conditions, credential scope, and network or filesystem boundaries are explicit.
Operator-visible telemetry, pause or kill paths, and audit evidence are documented when autonomy increases.
Prompt-injection, untrusted input, and privilege escalation paths are reviewed separately from implementation convenience.

stop_conditions: Autonomy boundaries and escalation paths are documented before implementation ships.
A reviewer can tell which actions are automatic, which require approval, and which are forbidden.
Release harness passes for any code or policy changes.
human_escalation: A change grants unattended write, network, credential, deployment, trading, or production access.
A sandbox, approval, or audit control cannot be mechanically enforced.

agent_team.rationale: Autonomy changes benefit from separating policy design, threat review, implementation, and operator-experience verification.
use_when: Use a policy reviewer for permission classes, sandbox boundaries, and credential scope.
Use a verifier agent to challenge prompt-injection, privilege escalation, and missing telemetry paths.
avoid_when: Stay single-agent only for a narrow documentation clarification that does not increase autonomy or touch controls.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep policy notes, risk review, runtime verifier output, and synthesis notes under autonomy evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Incident Triage
workflow_classes.purpose: Classify, dedupe, investigate, and escalate operational evidence without losing chronology or source quality.
use_when: The task involves logs, alerts, tickets, or incident reports.
Multiple symptoms may share a root cause.
Investigation needs bounded loops and escalation thresholds.

rubric: Symptoms are deduped before root-cause claims.
Log and metric evidence is bounded by time, source, and service.
Escalation conditions are explicit.

stop_conditions: Root cause is proven or the next diagnostic gap is explicit.
No unbounded log scan remains.
Release harness passes for any code changes.
human_escalation: Production credentials or raw sensitive logs are needed.
A remediation requires deployment, migration, or owner approval.

agent_team.rationale: Incident work sometimes benefits from parallel hypothesis checks, but live chronology and operational context can make one lead safer.
use_when: Use multiple agents for independent hypotheses, log families, or suspected services after time bounds are clear.
Use a verifier agent before claiming root cause or release readiness.
avoid_when: Avoid parallel agents when the incident is actively changing, credentials are sensitive, or chronology must stay under one operator.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep hypothesis evidence, rejected paths, and synthesis notes under incident evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Exploration Tournament
workflow_classes.purpose: Explore competing names, designs, architectures, or strategies and select against a rubric.
use_when: The task has multiple plausible qualitative answers.
A single path chosen too early would reduce quality.
The winner needs reasons, tradeoffs, and close runner-up comparisons.

rubric: Candidates are judged against the same criteria.
The selected option explains why it beats close alternatives.
Any implementation still passes the release harness.

stop_conditions: A winner and near-miss alternatives are recorded.
The judging rubric has no unaddressed hard requirement.
Release harness passes when code changed.
human_escalation: The choice encodes a business decision not in the spec.
No candidate satisfies a hard constraint.

agent_team.rationale: Alternative generation and rubric judging improve when candidate authors and judges are separated.
use_when: Use candidate agents to generate independent options.
Use a judge or verifier agent to apply the same rubric across options.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team when the choice is already constrained by a single approved spec or owner decision.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep candidates, rubric judgments, and synthesis notes under tournament evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Long-run Task Graph
workflow_classes.purpose: Keep multi-session or long-running agent work explicit, resumable, and checkable.
use_when: The task cannot be finished reliably in one context window.
Multiple dependent tasks need durable status, blockers, and evidence.
Compaction, handoff, or fresh-session restarts are expected.

rubric: Every task has status, owner or agent, evidence, blocker, and next action.
Handoff notes let a fresh session continue without guessing.
Stop conditions are tied to objective completion rather than elapsed time.

stop_conditions: All required task nodes are complete, deferred with owner approval, or blocked with evidence.
Fresh-session handoff can locate current state and next action.
Release harness passes for any code changes.
human_escalation: A blocker repeats without new evidence.
Task graph scope expands beyond the approved objective.

agent_team.rationale: Long work may need multiple agents, but the task graph and handoff discipline matter more than agent count.
use_when: Use agents for independent graph nodes with clear owners, inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.
Use a synthesis owner to merge node evidence into the durable ledger.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team when dependencies are strongly sequential or the ledger is not yet stable enough to delegate safely.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, tie per-agent evidence and synthesis notes to task graph nodes.

workflow_classes.name: Runtime Legibility
workflow_classes.purpose: Make the running system observable, controllable, and verifiable by agents and humans.
use_when: The change affects local startup, dev environments, UI feedback, observability, logs, metrics, traces, or smoke tests.
Agents need to inspect the app while it is running.
A project lacks deterministic commands for booting, probing, or debugging.

rubric: An agent can discover how to start and probe the system.
Logs, metrics, traces, or UI feedback identify failures without private knowledge.
Runtime evidence is captured under .artifacts/ when relevant.

stop_conditions: Documented run and smoke commands work or blockers are explicit.
Runtime signals expose enough context to diagnose the changed path.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: Production credentials, private hosts, or unsafe network access are needed.
The runtime contract would become cloud-provider-specific.

agent_team.rationale: Runtime work can split into docs, tooling, and smoke verification, but often needs one agent to preserve local environment context.
use_when: Use separate agents for environment setup review, smoke-path validation, and observability surface review when they can run independently.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team when local runtime state is fragile, credentials are involved, or only one agent can safely hold the session.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep probe logs, review notes, and synthesis notes under runtime evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Eval Improvement Loop
workflow_classes.purpose: Convert repeated agent, prompt, workflow, or review failures into durable checks.
use_when: An agent repeatedly makes the same class of mistake.
A prompt, skill, hook, or workflow needs measurable improvement.
A previous review or release escaped a defect that should become a regression check.

rubric: The original failure is captured as an example or regression case.
The new check fails before the fix and passes after the fix when feasible.
Trajectory review confirms the process improvement, not only the final diff.

stop_conditions: The regression check, eval, hook, or script covers the failure class.
Verifier rerun finds no remaining high-impact escape.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: The failure example includes secrets or sensitive production data.
The improvement would weaken an existing gate.

agent_team.rationale: Eval loops benefit from separating failure reproduction, fix generation, and verifier rerun, but tiny regression fixes can stay linear.
use_when: Use separate agents for trajectory review, regression design, candidate fix generation, and verifier rerun when failure modes are independent.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team when the failure is a single deterministic check with an obvious one-line fix.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep failure examples, verifier output, and synthesis notes under eval evidence.

workflow_classes.name: Skill Evolution
workflow_classes.purpose: Turn repeated workflows into focused skills, references, scripts, or plugins without bloating instructions.
use_when: A workflow is repeated often enough to deserve a reusable skill or script.
A skill needs new references, gotchas, trigger descriptions, or deterministic helpers.
Instruction bloat or prompt-cache churn is making agent behavior worse.

rubric: The skill has a narrow job and clear trigger boundaries.
References or scripts are loaded only when needed.
Trigger tests or reviewer prompts prove the intended invocation behavior.

stop_conditions: Skill entry point is concise and points to required references.
Trigger behavior and deterministic helpers are verified.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: The skill would encode project authority outside specs, docs, or scripts.
A plugin distribution or external dependency decision is required.

agent_team.rationale: Skill work can use reviewers for trigger boundaries and examples, but the entry point should stay owned by one editor to avoid instruction bloat.
use_when: Use review agents to test trigger behavior, progressive disclosure, and gotchas across examples.
avoid_when: Avoid Agent Team for a small skill text edit where one editor and one validator are enough.
evidence: Record the Agent Team decision; when used, keep trigger-test results, reviewer notes, and synthesis notes under skill evidence.
